Title: Fusaric acid instigates the invasion of banana by Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. cubense TR4

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes an essential early step in fusaric acid biosynthesis; exact reaction not defined in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for biosynthesis of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytotoxin and positive virulence factor that promotes banana invasion by Foc TR4 through mitochondrial dysfunction, ROS accumulation, cell death, and enhanced lesion formation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fungal culture; banana roots; banana pseudostem xylem sap
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. cubense tropical race 4
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected during culture; upregulated from day 3 to day 6 in WT.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fungal culture on PDA; banana infection; exogenous FSA rescue treatment (100 μM per plantlet) restored mutant invasion.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted gene deletion; complementation; qRT-PCR; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling in culture and infected plants; pathogenicity assay; fungal biomass qPCR.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deletion of FUB1 reduced fusaric acid production to ~3% of WT in culture. 2. Fusaric acid in infected banana tissues fell below or near detection, and virulence and in planta fungal biomass decreased. 3. Complementation restored fusaric acid production and pathogenicity, supporting FUB1 as essential for fusaric acid-mediated virulence.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes fusaric acid accumulation and thereby enhances fungal virulence in banana.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of FSA-treated banana ECSs, GenBank accession SAMN11155476
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion reduced virulence but did not affect fungal morphology or vegetative growth on PDA.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antifungal target to reduce fusaric acid production and banana wilt virulence; pathway inhibition may weaken pathogen invasion without obvious fungal growth defects in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FUB1 was one of the most critical FUB genes; mutant showed strong loss of FSA and delayed disease development.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a contributing step in fusaric acid biosynthesis; exact reaction not defined in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for full biosynthesis of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytotoxin and positive virulence factor that promotes banana invasion by Foc TR4 through mitochondrial dysfunction, ROS accumulation, cell death, and enhanced lesion formation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fungal culture; banana roots; banana pseudostem xylem sap
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. cubense tropical race 4
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected during culture; upregulated from day 3 to day 6 in WT.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fungal culture on PDA; banana infection; exogenous FSA rescue treatment (100 μM per plantlet) restored mutant invasion.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted gene deletion; complementation; qRT-PCR; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling in culture and infected plants; pathogenicity assay; fungal biomass qPCR.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deletion of FUB2 reduced fusaric acid production to ~30% of WT in culture. 2. Very low fusaric acid accumulated in infected roots and virulence was reduced. 3. Complementation restored defects, showing FUB2 contributes substantially to fusaric acid biosynthesis and virulence.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports full fusaric acid accumulation and promotes fungal virulence in banana.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of FSA-treated banana ECSs, GenBank accession SAMN11155476
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion reduced virulence but did not affect fungal morphology or vegetative growth on PDA.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate pathogen metabolic target for reducing toxin-assisted infection.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FUB2 mutant retained partial FSA production, indicating a contributing but not absolutely essential step.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes an essential step in fusaric acid biosynthesis; exact reaction not defined in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for biosynthesis of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytotoxin and positive virulence factor that promotes banana invasion by Foc TR4 through mitochondrial dysfunction, ROS accumulation, cell death, and enhanced lesion formation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fungal culture; banana roots; banana pseudostem xylem sap
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. cubense tropical race 4
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected during culture; upregulated from day 3 to day 6 in WT.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fungal culture on PDA; banana infection; exogenous FSA rescue treatment (100 μM per plantlet) restored mutant invasion.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted gene deletion; complementation; qRT-PCR; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling in culture and infected plants; pathogenicity assay; fungal biomass qPCR.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deletion of FUB3 reduced fusaric acid production to ~5% of WT in culture. 2. Fusaric acid in infected banana tissues was nearly abolished and virulence/fungal biomass were reduced. 3. Complementation restored toxin production and pathogenicity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes fusaric acid biosynthesis and toxin-dependent virulence in banana.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of FSA-treated banana ECSs, GenBank accession SAMN11155476
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion reduced virulence but did not affect fungal morphology or vegetative growth on PDA.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for disease control by suppressing pathogen toxin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FUB3 was highlighted in phylogeny/intergenic variation discussion, but only its deletion phenotype is core evidence here.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes an essential step in fusaric acid biosynthesis; exact reaction not defined in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for biosynthesis of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytotoxin and positive virulence factor that promotes banana invasion by Foc TR4 through mitochondrial dysfunction, ROS accumulation, cell death, and enhanced lesion formation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fungal culture; banana roots; banana pseudostem xylem sap
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. cubense tropical race 4
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected during culture; upregulated from day 3 to day 6 in WT.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fungal culture on PDA; banana infection; exogenous FSA rescue treatment (100 μM per plantlet) restored mutant invasion.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted gene deletion; complementation; qRT-PCR; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling in culture and infected plants; pathogenicity assay; fungal biomass qPCR.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deletion of FUB4 reduced fusaric acid production to ~1% of WT in culture. 2. Fusaric acid in infected tissues was undetectable or near undetectable and virulence was reduced. 3. Complementation restored the WT phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes fusaric acid accumulation and banana virulence.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of FSA-treated banana ECSs, GenBank accession SAMN11155476
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion reduced virulence but did not affect fungal morphology or vegetative growth on PDA.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highly promising pathogen target because knockout nearly abolished toxin production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among tested genes, FUB4 deletion caused one of the strongest FSA reductions.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a contributing step in fusaric acid biosynthesis; exact reaction not defined in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for full biosynthesis of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytotoxin and positive virulence factor that promotes banana invasion by Foc TR4 through mitochondrial dysfunction, ROS accumulation, cell death, and enhanced lesion formation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fungal culture; banana roots; banana pseudostem xylem sap
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. cubense tropical race 4
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected during culture; upregulated from day 3 to day 6 in WT.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fungal culture on PDA; banana infection; exogenous FSA rescue treatment (100 μM per plantlet) restored mutant invasion.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted gene deletion; complementation; qRT-PCR; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling in culture and infected plants; pathogenicity assay; fungal biomass qPCR.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deletion of FUB5 reduced fusaric acid production to ~50% of WT in culture. 2. Root tissues still contained very low FSA, but virulence and fungal biomass were significantly reduced. 3. Complementation restored the phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to full fusaric acid production and supports pathogenicity.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of FSA-treated banana ECSs, GenBank accession SAMN11155476
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion reduced virulence but did not affect fungal morphology or vegetative growth on PDA.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible metabolic control point for partially attenuating toxin production and disease.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FUB5 mutant retained the highest residual FSA among tested mutants, indicating partial redundancy or a less central step.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes an essential later step in fusaric acid biosynthesis; exact reaction not defined in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytotoxin and positive virulence factor that promotes banana invasion by Foc TR4 through mitochondrial dysfunction, ROS accumulation, cell death, and enhanced lesion formation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fungal culture; banana roots; banana pseudostem xylem sap
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. cubense tropical race 4
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript levels showed a sustained increase during the 9-day culture time course in WT.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fungal culture on PDA; banana infection; exogenous FSA rescue treatment (100 μM per plantlet) restored mutant invasion.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted gene deletion; complementation; qRT-PCR; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling in culture and infected plants; pathogenicity assay; fungal biomass qPCR.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FUB10 expression increased steadily during culture in WT. 2. Deletion of FUB10 reduced fusaric acid production to ~4% of WT in culture and nearly abolished accumulation in planta. 3. The mutant had reduced virulence and fungal biomass, while complementation restored both traits.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes late fusaric acid biosynthesis and is required for full toxin-dependent virulence.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of FSA-treated banana ECSs, GenBank accession SAMN11155476
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion reduced virulence but did not affect fungal morphology or vegetative growth on PDA.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate pathogen target because its disruption sharply reduces toxin production and pathogenicity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FUB10 was the only tested gene with sustained transcript increase across the culture time course.
